item.title: Install with conda
item.description: Install a recent version of minconda. First steo is to find latest version of r-base in the r channel. Don’t use the conda-forge versions. conda search -c r r-base Lets asume it is version 3.6.1, which was the latest at the time of writing. Create a new empty environment with this version number as part of the environment name. conda create -n r_361 conda activate r_361 Configure the search path in this new enviornment

item.title: tmux cheatsheet
item.description: Default Prefix Default prefix is Ctrl-b. Windows tmux new-window tmux neww tmux neww -n windowname tmux new -s sessionname -n windowname prefix c create new window prefix , rename window prefix n|p and prefix Ctrl n|Ctrl p go to next/previous window prefix 1|2… go to window # Panes prefix v|h vertical or horisontal pane split prefix spacebar cycle through default pane layouts. Default layout splits panes evenly. Use this to quickly make one big and one small pane.

item.title: Docker & create-react-app
item.description: CONTENT NOT RENDERED Only insert details here if you want to enter any additional detail about the purpose of the redirect, otherwise you can just leave this portion of your file blank.

item.title: create-react-app & Docker
item.description: These instructions are not perfect, but they work for the small webapps I create. Installation npx create-react-app my-app cd my-app rm -r node_modules dockerfile.dev used to host a dev server, and run tests. Just use docker-compose up to start. No need to manually build container. FROMnode:lts-alpineRUN mkdir -p /home/node/app/node_modules \ && chown -R node:node /home/node/appWORKDIR/home/node/appUSERnodeCOPY package.json .COPY yarn.lock .RUN yarn installCOPY --chown=node:node . .EXPOSE3000CMD ["yarn", "start"]dockerfile Build the webapp in a temporary container, and then create a production nginx server to host the app.

item.title: Using dockerfiles with a python virtualenv
item.description: Why a virtualenv in a container? Normally not needed as the container image itself is already a new clean environment. But I needed a multi-stage build where the python env should be created inside the builder and then copied over to the final image. A virtualenv gives a single directory with all of its dependencies that is easy to copy Dockerfile FROM python:3.8-slim as bulder ENV VIRTUAL_ENV=/opt/venv RUN python3 -m venv $VIRTUAL_ENV ENV PATH="$VIRTUAL_ENV/bin:$PATH" # Install dependencies: COPY requirements.

item.title: Docker Links
item.description: Dockerfile Running Docker Containers as Current Host User https://containo.us/blog/traefik-2-tls-101-23b4fbee81f1/

item.title: Git Links
item.description: merging How to make Git preserve specific files while merging

item.title: Kubernetes Links
item.description: Kubernetes Implementations Ubuntu MicroK8s Software Pakaging Helm.sh - The package manager for Kubernetes

item.title: Mermaid charts
item.description: Shortcodes Mermaid charts are created using Hugo shortcodes. Here is a simple example. {{< mermaid align="left" >}} graph LR Start --> Stop {{< /mermaid >}} graph LR Start -- Stop See more at https://mermaid-js.github.io/mermaid/#/

item.title: SSH setup for GitHub
item.description: Creating a new key I use the names id_rsa_github and and id_rsa_github.pub for my keys, using a unique key from evey host that I need to connect from. Use a keepass long random password. ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github To add to the ssh agent use: gpg-connect-agent UPDATESTARTUPTTY /bye ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github ssh-add -L Configuring GitGub Open the user settings pagem and find SSH and GPG keys on the left.

item.title: Cloudflare DNS Settings
item.description: Links https://www.cloudflare.com/learning/dns/what-is-1.1.1.1/ https://1.1.1.1/ ipv4 addresses 1.1.1.1 1.0.0.1 ipv6 addresses 2606:4700:4700::1111 2606:4700:4700::1001

item.title: Linux Open Ports
item.description: lsof sudo lsof -i -P -n | grep LISTEN -i: list open ports -P: dont convert port numbers to names -n: don’t do reverse dns lookups ss netstat has been deprecated. ss is its replacement. sudo ss -tulpn | grep LISTEN -t: tcp -u: udp -l: listenng sockets -p: process id of listening process -n: don’t do reverse dns lookups

item.title: About page at root level
item.description: Overview I am an article at /content/en/about.md. Pages at root level aren’t displayed in the homepage, they should be directly linked to like this one.
